title: Fragrant Sour Moroccan Rye Khlea
description: A plant-based friendly Moroccan dish featuring long-fermentation rye dough, with a sour and cheesy pull, perfect for a brunch mood. It is made with a combination of rye flour, olive oil, and spices.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the rye flour, olive oil, yeast, salt, sugar, and ras el hanout.
Gradually add the water to the dry ingredients and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a warm place for 24 hours.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a round khlea shape.
Place the khlea on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Sprinkle the vegan cheese shreds on top of the khlea.
Bake the khlea for 35 minutes until it is golden brown and the cheese is melted and pulled.
Remove the khlea from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
